$description: Semantic motion - Contextual animation tokens with productive/expressive modes

productive.description: Efficient, task-focused motion for dashboards, forms, and repeated interactions

entrance.rationale: Minimal delay - information arrives when needed

exit.rationale: Quick removal - gets out of the way fast

tight.rationale: Minimal cascade - functional rhythm without ceremony

expressive.description: Engaging, moment-focused motion for marketing, heroes, and storytelling

entrance.rationale: Intentional delay creates anticipation - feels choreographed

exit.rationale: Exit faster than entrance but with grace

comfortable.rationale: Clear rhythm - each element gets attention

utility.description: Context-agnostic motion patterns that adapt based on use

focus.accessibility: Zero delay for accessibility - users need immediate feedback
focus.wcagNote: WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.7 - Focus must be immediately visible

expand.rationale: Expansion slightly slower - gives content time to be noticed

useProductive: Component appears more than 3 times per session
User is focused on completing a task
Examples: dashboards, forms, tables, filters, settings
useExpressive: Examples: hero sections, feature showcases, testimonials, CTAs

mixing.rule: Within a single view, pick ONE mode and stick with it
mixing.exception: Marketing site nav can use productive (frequent) while hero uses expressive (spotlight)
mixing.antiPattern: Don't mix theatrical dashboard tables with instant marketing buttons - feels schizophrenic

reducedMotion.implementation: Set all durations to 0.01ms when user prefers reduced motion
reducedMotion.criticalNote: Some users experience vestibular disorders, migraines, or seizures from motion

maxDuration.productive: 200ms max - anything longer frustrates task-focused users
maxDuration.expressive: 500ms max - even theatrical motion should respect user time
maxDuration.exception: Page transitions and loading states can exceed 500ms if showing progress

essentialInformation.rule: Never rely solely on motion to convey critical information
essentialInformation.example: Don't just animate a success state - also show icon + color + text confirmation

variants.productiveEntrance: const variants = { hidden: { opacity: 0, y: 8 }, visible: { opacity: 1, y: 0, transition: { duration: 0.15, ease: [0.2, 0, 0.38, 0.9] } } }
variants.expressiveEntrance: const variants = { hidden: { opacity: 0, y: 24 }, visible: { opacity: 1, y: 0, transition: { duration: 0.4, ease: [0.4, 0.14, 0.3, 1], delay: 0.2 } } }

cardHover.usage: Subtle lift - indicates interactivity without distraction

inputFocus.usage: Instant ring - critical for keyboard accessibility

buttonPress.usage: Noticeable scale - feels tactile and responsive

cardHover.usage: Significant lift - dramatic hover state for marketing

$designPrinciples.duality: Motion serves two masters - efficiency (machine) and emotion (human). Choose deliberately.
$designPrinciples.purposeful: Every animation must justify its existence - guide attention, provide feedback, or establish hierarchy
$designPrinciples.contextual: Dashboard button hover ≠ hero CTA hover. Same element, different contexts, different motion.
$designPrinciples.performant: Animate only transform and opacity - avoid layout shifts at all costs
$designPrinciples.respectful: Always provide reduced-motion alternatives - motion can cause physical discomfort
$designPrinciples.consistent: Within a mode (productive/expressive), all similar interactions use the same timing

gpuAccelerated.rationale: Transform and opacity bypass layout/paint - 60fps guaranteed

willChange.usage: Add will-change: transform, opacity; for elements that WILL animate
willChange.warning: Creates compositing layer - uses GPU memory. Don't overuse.
willChange.remove: Remove will-change after animation completes to free resources

concurrentAnimations.productive: Max 5 simultaneous animations - more causes jank
concurrentAnimations.expressive: Max 8 simultaneous animations - storytelling requires more
concurrentAnimations.rule: Stagger instead of simultaneous when possible

budgets.rationale: Users tolerate longer sequences when expecting a show
